caveats: No prices, market-state paths, scenario states, or valuation outputs are generated in this fixture.
Reported values are placeholder/reference amounts for downstream simulation setup, not market data.

instruments.display_name: Public AI Sleeve Money Market Sweep

intentional_stories.description: Manager A, Manager B, and Manager F intentionally share synthetic compute, chip, and AI infrastructure exposure.
intentional_stories.title: AI and semiconductor overlap across managers

intentional_stories.description: Manager E owns cash, T-bills, short duration bonds, and defensive ballast that may look weak in raw P&L during risk-on periods.
intentional_stories.title: Liquidity reserve may lag but serves a mandate

intentional_stories.description: Manager D contains stale private marks, opaque fund interests, and data center investments requiring proxy or human-review treatment later.
intentional_stories.title: Private and opaque positions need confidence review

intentional_stories.description: The universe includes data center, grid, power campus, LNG, uranium, copper, and energy positions.
intentional_stories.title: Data center power and energy bottleneck exposure

managers.expected_contribution: Long-term growth engine; may create concentration and drawdown risk.
managers.intended_role: High-conviction public growth and AI infrastructure exposure.
managers.strategy_summary: Owns synthetic compute, semiconductor, cloud, data-center, and grid-enablement names.

managers.expected_contribution: Core compounding with less thematic concentration than Manager A.
managers.intended_role: Durable core public equity exposure with quality and valuation discipline.
managers.strategy_summary: Balances quality compounders, broad equity, and selective AI overlap.

managers.display_name: Manager C - Income and Cash Generation
managers.expected_contribution: Cash yield and portfolio income, with rate and credit spread sensitivity.
managers.intended_role: Income, credit, and rate-sensitive exposure for spending support.
managers.strategy_summary: Owns public credit, municipal income, floating-rate exposure, and income-oriented real assets.

managers.display_name: Manager D - Private Markets / Real Assets
managers.expected_contribution: Private-market return and inflation-sensitive exposure with lower transparency.
managers.intended_role: Illiquid growth, real assets, private credit, and data center exposure.
managers.strategy_summary: Holds synthetic fund interests, real estate, infrastructure, data centers, and opaque private marks.

managers.display_name: Manager E - Liquidity Reserve / Defensive Ballast
managers.expected_contribution: May lag in risk-on markets but supports liquidity and drawdown control.
managers.intended_role: Liquidity, capital preservation, and spending buffer.
managers.strategy_summary: Holds cash, Treasury bills, short-duration bonds, defensive ETFs, and inflation hedges.

managers.expected_contribution: Crisis convexity and differentiated macro exposure, with more complex valuation needs.
managers.intended_role: Macro hedges, currency exposure, commodities, crypto, and option-like risk controls.
managers.strategy_summary: Uses synthetic FX, commodity, volatility, crypto, and structured exposures.

portfolio.description: Fictional multi-manager portfolio built to exercise manager role review, theme exposure, private-market confidence, liquidity, and future valuation workflows.

themes.description: Compute, cloud, data center, and grid enablement exposure.
Chip design, manufacturing, equipment, and semiconductor ETFs.
Data center, power, grid, and cooling requirements.
Energy supply, power bottlenecks, and commodity constraints.
Duration, curve, credit spread, and rate-proxy exposure.
Illiquid private funds, real assets, and secondary liquidity limits.
Income, distributions, dividends, coupons, and spending support.
Style classification for growth, value, or balanced exposure.
Liquidity, short duration, minimum volatility, and stabilizing assets.
Shared or shadowed exposures across managers.
Inflation-linked, commodity, real asset, and pricing power exposure.
USD liquidity, USD strength, and currency hedge exposure.
Bitcoin, crypto infrastructure, and digital asset volatility exposure.
Private, stale, low-transparency, or manager-level valuation marks.
Positions with incomplete, stale, proxy, or human-review valuation needs.
